pattern: Labelling each existing island with a unique id and its size, then for every empty cell summing the sizes of its distinct neighbouring islands plus one, finds the best single flip.
hints: First measure the islands that already exist; flipping a cell can only ever merge the islands touching that cell.
Give every island a distinct id and record its area, then for each zero look at its up-to-four neighbours and add the areas of the distinct island ids around it.
The answer is the largest neighbour-area sum plus one over all zeros, or the whole grid if there are no zeros to flip.

edge_inputs.description: first example, flipping joins two singletons
second example, one flip extends an L shape
third example, no zero to flip, whole grid is the island
single zero cell becomes size one
all water, the one flip yields size one
central zero bridges four separate islands
flip touches the same island twice, count it once

stress.description: random binary grid with a moderate density of ones
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 40))
    grid = [[1 if rng.random() < 0.55 else 0 for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: grid capped so the brute-force oracle stays well under the time limit
